c64b6e0b-a6f0-409c-810c-c9a87fb3fb75.theory: EUR_USD exhibits intraday momentum during active trading sessions (6-16 UTC), particularly during European and US overlap periods, making breakout strategies viable during these hours.
c64b6e0b-a6f0-409c-810c-c9a87fb3fb75.results_reasoning: The strategy generated zero trades because the entry conditions were too restrictive, requiring the current close to break above/below the previous bar's high/low, which is an extremely rare occurrence in normal market conditions.
learnings: Breakout conditions using close > previous high are too stringent for H1 timeframes and rarely trigger
Entry logic should use high/low of current bar rather than close for breakout detection
Time-based filters alone are insufficient if the core entry logic is fundamentally flawed
Always validate entry conditions on historical data before implementing complex position management

058eb5ff-96a9-4a4b-af72-f41d22068e5f.theory: EUR_USD exhibits short-term momentum patterns where strong directional moves (close significantly above/below open) tend to continue in the same direction for several hours.
058eb5ff-96a9-4a4b-af72-f41d22068e5f.results_reasoning: The strategy failed with negative Sharpe ratio and low win rate because momentum signals based solely on single-bar close-open relationships are too noisy and don't reliably predict continuation patterns in EUR_USD hourly timeframe.
learnings: Single-bar momentum signals (close vs open) are insufficient for reliable trend continuation prediction
EUR_USD hourly momentum requires multi-bar confirmation or additional filters to reduce false signals
24-hour maximum hold time may be too long for capturing short-term momentum bursts
Win rate below 40% suggests the momentum hypothesis needs stronger entry criteria or different market conditions
